skill.name: skill-creator
skill.description: Guide and tooling for creating, validating, and packaging AI skills with clear instructions, resources, and reusable workflows.
skill.summary: Create, validate, and package reusable AI skills with guided structure and examples.
skill.author: Azeem-2

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high severity patterns, but review found the weak cryptography, network, and sensitive-key detections are false positives from license text, markdown examples, and validation wording. The remaining real risk is moderate: included Python scripts create directories, write files, change permissions, and package every file under a selected skill folder.

medium_findings.title: Filesystem-Modifying Helper Scripts
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The initializer creates directories, writes multiple files, and marks an example script executable. This is legitimate skill scaffolding, but users should run it only in intended output locations.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly writes files and changes permissions in a user-selected path. The behavior is intentional and bounded to skill scaffolding, so the risk is moderate rather than high.

medium_findings.title: Packaging Includes All Files Under Skill Folder
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE. The packager recursively writes every file under the selected skill directory into a distributable archive. This can unintentionally include secrets or private assets if the source folder is not reviewed first.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The recursive package loop is clear and there is no filtering of secret-like files. The issue depends on user-provided folder contents, so it is a publication warning rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: Static Network Detections Are License URLs
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The detected URLs are Apache License reference links. There is no code path that performs a network request or sends data externally.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are standard Apache License text. No executable networking API appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The reported weak cryptography locations are normal prose, markdown examples, or validation strings. No hashing, encryption, signing, or password handling implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed contexts contain documentation and validation constants, not cryptographic APIs. Static matches appear to trigger on common words such as description, license, or metadata.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Detections Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. Most external command detections are fenced markdown examples or inline command names that explain how to initialize, validate, and package skills.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations are documentation blocks, not runtime shell execution. The actual Python scripts do not invoke subprocesses or shell commands.

low_findings.title: Sensitive-Key and Reconnaissance Detections Are Validation Wording
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE. The sensitive-key detection refers to checking unexpected YAML keys, and reconnaissance detections refer to standard imports or validation parsing. No credential collection or host probing was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code validates SKILL.md frontmatter with local parsing only. There is no credential file access, environment secret access, or system inventory behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Archive Packaging Without Exclusion Rules
dangerous_patterns.description: The packager archives every file found by recursive traversal. This is useful for distribution, but users must remove credentials, private data, and unnecessary files before packaging.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The traversal and zip write are explicit. The risk is contextual because sensitive files are only included if already present in the selected folder.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent guidance when turning repeated AI workflows into reusable skills. This skill provides structure, examples, and scripts for creating, validating, and packaging skills.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to structure a SKILL.md file with clear frontmatter and concise body instructions.
Guides users through planning scripts, references, and assets for reusable AI workflows.
Provides an initializer script that creates a new skill folder with example resources.
Provides a validator script for required frontmatter and naming rules.
Provides a packager script that creates a distributable .skill archive after validation.
Includes reference guides for workflow patterns and output format patterns.
limitations: The scripts modify local files and should be run only in intended directories.
The packager includes all files in the chosen skill folder unless users remove them first.
It does not prove that a generated skill is useful or secure for every deployment.
It focuses on general skill design, not one specific domain or API.

use_cases.title: Create a New Workflow Skill
use_cases.description: Turn a repeated team process into a structured skill with instructions, resources, and validation steps.

use_cases.title: Package a Skill for Sharing
use_cases.description: Validate a completed skill folder and produce a .skill archive for distribution.

use_cases.title: Improve Existing Skill Design
use_cases.description: Refine instructions, move long references out of SKILL.md, and clarify when resources should load.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Basic Skill
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me plan a skill for this repeated workflow: [describe workflow]. Suggest the SKILL.md structure and needed resources.

prompt_templates.title: Create Skill Files
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the skill creator workflow to initialize a skill named [skill-name] in [directory]. Then explain what files I should customize.

prompt_templates.title: Review Skill Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this skill folder for clarity, progressive disclosure, unnecessary files, and reusable resource design. Recommend focused changes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare for Marketplace Publication
prompt_templates.prompt: Validate and package this skill for sharing. Check for missing metadata, overly broad instructions, unnecessary files, and sensitive content before packaging.

output_examples.input: I want a skill that helps create weekly product status updates.
output: Recommended structure: overview, workflow steps, status format, and reference examples.
Suggested resources: a reference guide for audience-specific tone and a reusable output template.
Next action: initialize the skill, then replace placeholder content with team-specific guidance.

output_examples.input: Check whether my skill is ready to package.
output: Validation focus: frontmatter, name format, description quality, and required SKILL.md file.
Packaging warning: remove private data, drafts, and unused assets before creating the archive.
Result expectation: a distributable skill file only after validation passes.

best_practices: Keep SKILL.md concise and move detailed reference material into clearly linked reference files.
Test included scripts with representative inputs before packaging or sharing a skill.
Review the final skill folder for secrets and unnecessary files before creating an archive.
anti_patterns: Do not place long background documents in SKILL.md when they can load as references.
Do not package generated examples, drafts, or private files that users should not receive.
Do not write broad trigger descriptions that activate the skill for unrelated tasks.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create AI skills with clear instructions, reusable resources, validation, and packaging steps.

faq.question: Does it create the full skill automatically?
faq.answer: It can scaffold a skill folder, but users still need to replace placeholders with real workflow guidance.

faq.question: Can it validate a skill before sharing?
faq.answer: Yes. The included validator checks frontmatter, required fields, naming rules, and description constraints.

faq.question: Can it package a skill?
faq.answer: Yes. The packager validates the folder and creates a .skill archive from the selected skill directory.

faq.question: What should users review before packaging?
faq.answer: Users should remove secrets, private files, unused examples, and any assets that should not be distributed.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
